author: Augustine of Hippo
book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.

ccel.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

source.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

title: Psalm XXXVI

source.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: 1. …“The ungodly hath said in himself that he will sin: there is no fear of God before his eyes” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Not of one man, but of a race of ungodly men he speaketh, who fight against their own selves, by not understanding, that so they may live well; not because they cannot, but because they will not.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For it is one thing, when one endeavours to understand some thing, and through infirmity of flesh cannot; as saith the Scripture in a certain place, “For the corruptible body presseth down the soul, and the earthly tabernacle weigheth down the mind that museth upon many things;” but another when the human heart acts mischievously against itself, so that what it could understand, if it had but good will thereto, it understandeth not, not because it is difficult, but because the will is contrary.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But so it is when men love their own sins, and hate God’s Commandments.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For the Word of God is thy adversary, if thou be a friend to thy ungodliness; but if thou art an adversary to thy ungodliness, the Word of God is thy friend, as well as the adversary of thy ungodliness.…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “For he hath wrought deceitfully in His sight” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: In whose sight?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: In His, whose fear was not before the eyes of him that did work deceitfully.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “To find out his iniquity, and hate it.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He wrought so as not to find it.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For there are men who as it were endeavour to seek out their iniquity, and fear to find it; because if they should find it, it is said to them, Depart from it: this thou didst before thou knewest; thou didst iniquity being in ignorance; God giveth pardon: now thou hast discovered it, forsake it, that to thy ignorance pardon may easily be given; and that with a clear face thou mayest say to God, “Remember not the sins of my youth, and of my ignorance.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thus he seeketh it, thus he feareth lest he find it; for he seeketh it deceitfully.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: When saith a man, I knew not that it was sin?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: When he hath seen that it is sin, and ceaseth to do the sin, which he did only because he was ignorant: such an one in truth would know his sin, to find it out, and hate it.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But now many “work deceitfully to find out their iniquity:” they work not from their heart to find it out and hate it.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But because in the very search after iniquity, there is deceit, in the finding it there will be defence of it.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For when one hath found his iniquity, lo now it is manifest to him that it is iniquity.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Do it not, thou sayest.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And he who wrought deceitfully to find it out, now he hath found, hateth it not; for what saith he?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: How many do this!

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Who is there that doth it not?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And will God destroy them all?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Or at least he saith this: if God would not these things to be done, would men live who commit the same?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Seest thou that thou didst work deceitfully to find out thy iniquity?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For if not deceitfully but sincerely thou hadst wrought, thou wouldest now have found it out, and hated it; now thou hast found it out, and thou defendest it; therefore thou didst work deceitfully, when thou soughtest it.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “The words of his mouth are iniquity and deceit: he would not understand, that he might do good” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Ye see that he attributeth that to the will: for there are men who would understand and cannot, and there are men who would not understand, and therefore understand not.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “He would not understand, that he might do good.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “He hath meditated iniquity on his bed.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What said He, “On his bed?” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “The ungodly hath said in himself, that he will sin:” what above he said, in himself, that here he said, “On his bed.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Our bed is our heart: there we suffer the tossing of an evil conscience; and there we rest when our conscience is good.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Whoso loveth the bed of his heart, let him do some good therein.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: There is our bed, where the Lord Jesus Christ commands us to pray.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Enter into thy chamber, and shut thy door.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is, “Shut thy door?”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Expect not from God such things as are without, but such as are within; “and thy Father which seeth in secret, shall reward thee openly.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Who is he that shutteth not the door?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He who asketh much from God such things, and in such wise directeth all his prayers, that he may receive the goods that are of this world.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thy door is open, the multitude seeth when thou prayest.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is it to shut thy door?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: To ask that of God, which God alone knoweth how He giveth.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is that for which thou prayest, when thou hast shut the door?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What “eye hath not seen, nor ear heard, or hath entered into the heart of man.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And haply it hath not entered into thy very bed, that is, into thy heart.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But God knoweth what He will give: but when shall it be?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: When the Lord shall be revealed, when the Judge shall appear.…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “He hath set himself in every way that is not good.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is, “he hath set himself”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He hath sinned perseveringly.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Whence also of a certain pious and good man it is said, “He hath not stood in the way of sinners.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: As this “hath not stood,” so that “hath set himself.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “But wickedness hath he not hated.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: There is the end, there the fruit: if a man cannot but have wickedness, let him at least hate it.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For when thou hatest it, it scarcely occurs to thee to do any wickedness.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For sin is in our mortal body, but what saith the Apostle?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Let not sin reign in your mortal body, that ye should obey it in the lusts thereof.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: When beginneth it not to be therein?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: When that shall be fulfilled in us which he saith, “When this corruptible shall have put on incorruption, and this mortal shall have put on immortality.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Before this come to pass, there is a delighting in sin in the body, but greater is the delighting and the pleasure in the Word of Wisdom, in the Commandment of God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Overcome sin and the lust thereof.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Sin and iniquity do thou hate, that thou mayest join thyself to God, who hateth it as well as thou.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Now being joined in mind unto the Law of God, in mind thou servest the Law of God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And if in the flesh thou therefore servest the law of sin, because there are in thee certain carnal delightings, then will there be none when thou shalt no longer fight.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: It is one thing not to fight, and to be in true and lasting peace; another to fight and overcome; another to fight and to be overcome; another not to fight at all, but to be carried away.…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Thy mercy, O Lord, is in the heavens, and Thy truth reacheth even unto the clouds” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: I know not what Mercy of Him he meaneth, which is in the heavens.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For the Mercy of the Lord is also in the earth.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thou hast it written, “The earth is full of the Mercy of the Lord.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Of what Mercy then speaketh He, when He saith, “Thy Mercy, O Lord, is in the heavens”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The gifts of God are partly temporal and earthly, partly eternal and heavenly.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Whoso for this worshippeth God, that he may receive those temporal and earthly goods, which are open to all, is still as it were like the brutes: he enjoyeth indeed the Mercy of God, but not that which is excepted, which shall not be given, save only to the righteous, to the holy, to the good.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What are the gifts which abound to all?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “He maketh His sun to rise on the evil and on the good, and sendeth rain on the just and on the unjust.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Who hath not this Mercy of God, first that he hath being, that he is distinguished from the brutes, that he is a rational animal, so as to understand God; secondly, that he enjoys this light, this air, rain, fruits, diversity of seasons, and all the earthly comforts, health of body, the affection of friends, the safety of his family?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: All these are good, and they are God’s gifts.…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But this man rightly understood what mercy he should pray for from God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Thy Mercy, O Lord, is in the Heavens; and Thy Truth reacheth even to the clouds.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: That is, the Mercy which Thou givest to Thy Saints, is Heavenly, not earthly; is Eternal, not temporal.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And how couldest Thou declare it unto men?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Because “Thy Truth reacheth even unto the clouds.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For who could know the Heavenly Mercy of God, unless God should declare it unto men?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: How did He declare it?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: By sending His truth even unto the clouds.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What are the clouds?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The Preachers of the Word of God.…Truth reached even to the clouds: therefore unto us could be declared the Mercy of God, which is in Heaven and not in earth.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And truly, Brethren, the clouds are the Preachers of the Word of Truth.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: When God threateneth through His Preachers, He thunders through the clouds.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: When God worketh miracles through His Preachers, He lightneth through the clouds, He terrifieth through the clouds, and watereth by the rain.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Those Preachers, then, by whom is preached the Gospel of God, are the clouds of God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let us then hope for Mercy, but for that which is in the Heavens.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Thy Righteousness is like the mountains of God: Thy Judgments are a great deep” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Who are the mountains of God?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Those who are called clouds, the same are also the mountains of God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The great Preachers are the mountains of God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And as when the sun riseth, he first clothes the mountains with light, and thence the light descends to the lowest parts of the earth: so our Lord Jesus Christ, when He came, first irradiated the height of the Apostles, first enlightened the mountains, and so His Light descended to the valley of the world.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And therefore saith He in a certain Psalm, “I lifted up mine eyes unto the mountains, from whence cometh my help.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But think not that the mountains themselves will give thee help: for they receive what they may give, give not of their own.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And if thou remain in the mountains, thy hope will not be strong: but in Him who enlighteneth the mountains, ought to be thy hope and presumption.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thy help indeed will come to thee through the mountains, because the Scriptures are administered to thee through the mountains, through the great Preachers of the Truth: but fix not thy hope in them.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Hear what He saith next following: “I lifted up mine eyes unto the mountains, from whence cometh my help.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Do the mountains give thee help?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: No; hear what follows, “My help cometh from the Lord, which made Heaven and earth.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Through the mountains cometh help, but not from the mountains.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: From whom then?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “From the Lord, which made Heaven and earth.”…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Thy Judgments are like the great abyss.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The abyss he calleth the depth of sin, whither every one cometh by despising God; as in a certain place it is said, “God gave them over to their own hearts’ lusts, to do the things which are not convenient.”…Because then they were proud and ungrateful, they were held worthy to be delivered up to the lusts of their own hearts, and became a great abyss, so that they not only sinned, but also worked craftily, lest they should understand their iniquity, and hate it.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: That is the depth of wickedness, to be unwilling to find it out and to hate it.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But how one cometh to that depth, see; “Thy Judgments are the great abyss.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: As the mountains are by the Righteousness of God, who through His Grace become great: so also through His Judgments come they unto the depth, who sink lowest.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: By this then let the mountains delight thee, by this turn away from the abyss, and turn thyself unto that, of which it is said, “My help cometh from the Lord.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But whereby?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “I have lifted up mine eyes unto the mountains.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What meaneth this?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: I will speak plainly.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: In the Church of God thou findest an abyss, thou findest also mountains; thou findest there but few good, because the mountains are few, the abyss broad; that is, thou findest many living ill after the wrath of God, because they have so worked that they are delivered up to the lusts of their own heart; so now they defend their sins and confess them not; but say, Why?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What have I done?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Such an one did this, and such an one did that.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Now will they even defend what the Divine Word reproves.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This is the abyss.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Therefore in a certain place saith the Scripture (hear this abyss), “The sinner when he cometh unto the depth of sin despiseth.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: See, “Thy Judgments are like the great abyss.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But yet not art thou a mountain; not yet art thou in the abyss; fly from the abyss, tend towards the mountains; but yet remain not on the mountains.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “For thy help cometh from the Lord, which made Heaven and earth.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Because he said, Thy Mercy is in the Heavens, that it may be known to be also on earth, he said, “O Lord, Thou savest man and beast, as Thy Mercy is multiplied, O God” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Great is Thy Mercy, and manifold is Thy Mercy, O God; and that showest Thou both to man and beast.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For from whom is the saving of men?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Is not the saving of beasts also from God?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For He who made man, made also beasts; He who made both, saveth both; but the saving of beasts is temporal.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But there are who as a great thing ask this of God, which He hath given to beasts.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Thy Mercy, O God, is multiplied,” so that not only unto men, but unto beasts also is given the same saving which is given to men, a carnal and temporal saving.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Have not men then somewhat reserved with God, which beasts deserve not, and whereunto beasts arrive not?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: They have evidently.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And where is that which they have.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “The children of men put their trust under the shadow of Thy wings.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Attend, my Beloved, to this most pleasant sentence; “Thou savest man and beast.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: First, he spake of “man and beast,” then of “the children of men;” as though “men” were one, “the children of men” other.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Sometimes in Scripture children of men is said generally of all men, sometimes in some proper manner, with some proper signification, so that not all men are understood; chiefly when there is a distinction.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For not without reason is it here put; “O Lord, Thou savest man and beast: but the children of men;” as though setting aside the first, he keepeth separate the children of men.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Separate from whom?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Not only from beasts, but also from men, who seek from God the saving of beasts, and desire this as a great thing.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Who then are the children of men?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Those who put their trust under the shadow of His wings.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For those men together with beasts rejoice in possession, but the children of men rejoice in hope: those follow after present goods with beasts, these hope for future goods with Angels.…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “They shall be satiated with the fulness of Thy House” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He promiseth us some great thing.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He would speak it, and He speaketh it not.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Can He not, or do not we receive it?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: I dare, my Brethren, to say, even of holy tongues and hearts, by which Truth is declared to us, that it can neither be spoken, which they declared, nor even thought of.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For it is a great thing, and ineffable; and even they saw through a glass darkly, as saith the Apostle, “For now we see through a glass darkly; but then face to face.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Lo, they who saw through a glass darkly, thus burst forth.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What then shall we be, when we shall see face to face?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: That with which they travailed in heart, and could not with their tongue bring forth, that men might receive it.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For what necessity was there that he should say, “They shall be satiated with the fulness of Thy House”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He sought a word whereby to express from human things what he would say; and because he saw that men drowning themselves in drunkenness receive indeed wine without measure, but lose their senses, he saw what to say; for when shall have been received that ineffable joy, then shall be lost in a manner the human soul, it shall become Divine, and be satiated with the fulness of God’s House.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Wherefore also in another Psalm it is said, “Thy cup inebriating, how excellent is it!”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: With this cup were the Martyrs satiated when going to their passion, they knew not their own.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What so inebriated as not to know a wife weeping, not children, not parents?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: They knew them not, they thought not that they were before their eyes.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Wonder not: they were inebriated.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Wherewith were they so?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Lo, they had received a cup wherewith they were satiated.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Wherefore he also gives thanks to God, saying, “What shall I render unto the Lord for all His benefits towards me?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: I will take the cup of Salvation, and call upon the Name of the Lord.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Therefore, Brethren of men, let us be children and let us trust under the shadow of His wings and be satiated with the fulness of His House.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: As I could, I have spoken; and as far as I can I see; and how far I see, I cannot speak.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And of the torrent of Thy Pleasure shalt Thou give them to drink.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: A torrent we call water coming with a flood.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: There will be a flood of God’s Mercy to overflow and inebriate those who now put their trust under the shadow of His wings.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is that Pleasure?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: As it were a torrent inebriating the thirsty.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let him then who thirsts now, lay up hope: whoso thirsts now, let him have hope; when inebriated, he shall have possession: before he have possession, let him thirst in hope.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Blessed are they which do hunger and thirst after righteousness, for they shall be filled.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: With what fountain then wilt thou be overflowed, and whence runneth such a torrent of His Pleasure?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “For with Thee,” saith he, “is the fountain of Life.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is the fountain of Life, but Christ?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He came to thee in the flesh, that He might bedew thy thirsty lips: He will satisfy thee trusting, who bedewed thee thirsting.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “For with Thee is the fountain of Life; in Thy Light shall we see light” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Here a fountain is one thing, light another: there not so.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For that which is the Fountain, the same is also Light: and whatever thou wilt thou callest It, for It is not what thou callest It: for thou canst not find a fit name: for It remaineth not in one name.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If thou shouldest say, that It is Light only, it would be said to thee, Then without cause am I told to hunger and thirst, for who is there that eateth light?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: It is said to me plainly, directly, “Blessed are the pure in heart: for they shall see God.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If It is Light, my eyes must I prepare.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Prepare also lips; for That which is Light is also a Fountain: a Fountain, because It satisfieth the thirsty: Light, because It enlighteneth the blind.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Here sometimes, light is in one place, a fountain in another.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For sometimes fountains run even in darkness; and sometimes in the desert thou sufferest the sun, findest no fountain: here then can these two be separated: there thou shalt not be wearied, for there is a Fountain; there thou shalt not be darkened, for there is Light.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Show forth Thy Mercy unto them that know Thee; Thy Righteousness to them that are of a right heart” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: As I have said, Those are of a right heart who follow in this life the Will of God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The will of God is sometimes that thou shouldest be whole, sometimes that thou shouldest be sick.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If when thou art whole God’s Will be sweet, and when thou art sick God’s Will be bitter; thou art not of a right heart.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Because thou wilt not make right thy will according to God’s Will, but wilt bend God’s Will to thine.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: That is right, but thou art crooked: thy will must be made right to That, not That made crooked to thee; and thou wilt have a right heart.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: It is well with thee in this world; be God blessed, who comforteth thee: it goeth hardly with thee in this world; be God blessed, because He chasteneth and proveth thee; and so wilt thou be of a right heart, saying, “I will bless the Lord at all times: His Praise shall be ever in my mouth.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Let not the foot of pride come against me” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But now he said, The children of men shall put their trust under the shadow of Thy wings: they shall be satiated with the fulness of Thy House.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: When one hath begun to be plentifully overflowed with that Fountain, let him take heed lest he grow proud.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For the same was not wanting to Adam, the first man: but the foot of pride came against him, and the hand of the sinner removed him, that is, the proud hand of the devil.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: As he who seduced him, said of himself, “I will sit in the sides of the north;” so he persuaded him, by saying, “Taste, and ye shall be as gods.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: By pride then have we so fallen as to arrive at this mortality.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And because pride had wounded us, humility maketh us whole.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: God came humbly, that from such great wound of pride He might heal man.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He came, for “The Word was made Flesh, and dwelt among us.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He was taken by the Jews; He was reviled of them.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Ye heard when the Gospel was read, what they said, and to Whom they said, “Thou hast a devil:” and He said not, Ye have a devil, for ye are still in your sins, and the devil possesseth your hearts.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He said not this, which if He had said, He had said truly: but it was not meet that He should say it, lest He should seem not to preach Truth, but to retort evil speaking.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He let go what He heard as though He heard it not.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For a Physician was He, and to cure the madman had He come.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: As a Physician careth not what he may hear from the madman; but how the madman may recover and become sane; nor even if he receive a blow from the madman, careth he; but while he to him giveth new wounds, he cureth his old fever: so also the Lord came to the sick man, to the madman came He, that whatever He might hear, whatever He might suffer, He should despise; by this very thing teaching us humility, that being taught by humility, we might be healed from pride: from which he here prayeth to be delivered, saying, “Let not the foot of pride come against me; neither let the hand of the sinner remove me.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For if the foot of pride come, the hand of the sinner removeth.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is the hand of the sinner?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The working of him that adviseth ill.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Hast thou become proud?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Quickly he corrupteth thee who adviseth ill.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Humbly fix thyself in God, and care not much what is said to thee.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Hence is that which is elsewhere spoken, “From my secret sins cleanse Thou me; and from others’ sins also keep Thy servant.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is, “From my secret sins”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Let not the foot of pride come against me.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is, “From other men’s sins also keep Thy servant”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Let not the hand of the wicked remove me.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Keep that which is within, and thou shalt not fear from without.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But wherefore so greatly fearest thou this?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Because it is said, “Thereby have fallen all that work iniquity” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: 12); so that they have come into that abyss of which it is said, “Thy judgments are like the great abyss:” so that they have come even to that deep wherein sinners who despise have fallen.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Have fallen.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Whereby did they first fall?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: By the foot of pride.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Hear the foot of pride.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “When they knew God, they glorified Him not as God.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Therefore came against them the foot of pride, whereby they came into the depth.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “God gave them over to their own hearts’ lusts, to do those things which are not convenient.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The root of sin, and the head of sin feared he who said, “Let not the foot of pride come against me.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Wherefore said he, “the foot”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Because by walking proudly man deserted God, and departed from Him.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: His foot, called he his affection.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Let not the foot of pride come against me: let not the hand of the wicked remove me:” that is, let not the works of the wicked remove me from Thee, that I should wish to imitate them.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But wherefore said he this against pride, “Thereby have fallen all that work iniquity”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Because those who now are ungodly, have fallen by pride.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Therefore when the Lord would caution His Church, He said, “It shall watch thy head, and thou shalt watch his heel.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The serpent watcheth when the foot of pride may come against thee, when thou mayest fall, that he may cast thee down.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But watch thou his head: the beginning of all sin is pride.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Thereby have fallen all that work iniquity: they are driven out, and are not able to stand.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He first, who in the Truth stood not, then, through him, they whom God sent out of Paradise.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Whence he, the humble, who said that he was not worthy to unloose His shoe’s latchet, is not driven out, but standeth and heareth Him, and rejoiceth greatly because of the Bridegroom’s voice; not because of his own, lest the foot of pride come against him, and he be driven out, and be not able to stand.…
